facts.text: Kenwood Estates first appeared in 1925 when Alfred H. advertised the “beautiful Pine Ridge of Military Trail,” promising a future of shopping centers, apartments, duplexes, and single‑family homes.
The census‑designated place stretches from Attleboro Avenue westward to Haverhill Road, south to the L‑11 Canal, and north along the south side of 10th Avenue North.

Since the early 2000s, the Countywide Community Revitalization Team has poured more than $2.7 million into Kenwood Estates, adding a park, sidewalks, speed bumps, streetlights, improved drainage and pavement.
During the revitalization, a community policing deputy was assigned to Kenwood Estates to help restore a sense of security.
Kenwood Estates sits at latitude 26.6276 and longitude –80.1152 in Palm Beach County.
